mechanism_narrative: A contemporaneous year-over-year increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects strengthening consumer expenditure, which translates into higher corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns is not positive and statistically significant (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates and debt-service costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in the unemployment rate and decrease in nonfarm payroll employment signals accelerating labor market deterioration that crushes household income growth and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls, the subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 45% of observations, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) in a bivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, a month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals binding supply constraints and peaking earnings momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle months with month-over-month INDPRO declines, 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or the mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,000 thousand units indicates a collapse in residential construction demand that spills over into durable goods and employment, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1000, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate coupled with accelerating consumer price inflation drives real interest rates higher and compresses equity valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL does not significantly predict negative 20-day returns (t-stat > -1.5) in a multivariate regression, or the joint condition yields positive mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and rising industrial profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns is not positive and statistically significant (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.5 percent signals a tight labor market that triggers wage-price spirals and broad-based margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE falls below 4.5%, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).
